text.title: Next.js App Router Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Next.js App Router application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Files in app/ are React Server Components by default. Add `'use client'` only when the component requires hooks, browser APIs, or event handlers.
rule.rationale: Defaulting client makes server-rendering benefits disappear; defaulting server preserves them and forces explicit decisions about interactivity.

rule.statement: `'use client'` is added at the smallest necessary boundary, not the page or layout top. Client-only widgets are isolated; their parents stay server.
rule.rationale: Top-level `'use client'` cascades client-only to all descendants; small boundaries preserve maximum server rendering.

rule.statement: Mutations triggered from forms or buttons go through server actions, not API routes. API routes are reserved for webhooks and third-party integrations.
rule.rationale: Server actions handle CSRF, progressive enhancement, and revalidation for free; rolling your own API + fetch path loses these benefits.

rule.statement: Each route segment that needs them defines loading.tsx (Suspense boundary) and error.tsx (error boundary). No bespoke spinner/banner per page.
rule.rationale: Per-segment conventions give consistent UX and let Next.js orchestrate streaming + recovery.

rule.statement: Client components don't `fetch` on mount; data is passed from server components or fetched via server actions. SWR/TanStack Query allowed only when justified by interactivity (live polling, subscriptions).
rule.rationale: fetch-on-mount triggers waterfalls and undoes server-rendering benefits; passing data from server is the canonical shape.

rule.statement: Each data read declares its cache strategy: `fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: N, tags: [t] } })` or `export const revalidate = N`. No implicit / default behaviour at scale.
rule.rationale: Implicit caching surprises engineers and breaks freshness assumptions; explicit declaration is the only auditable shape.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
